problemStatement: Chester runs pipeline-shaped but works loop-shaped: implementation-surfaced decisions never propagate back to spec, tests, or future plans — within a sprint or across sprints.

EVID-1.statement: Chester runs as a one-directional pipeline: design → spec → plan → execute → finish → archive. Each stage feeds the next; no stage reads back into upstream artifacts during normal operation.

EVID-2.statement: finish-write-records captures decisions retrospectively by parsing the session JSONL transcript at sprint end. The reasoning-audit artifact lands in summary/ and does not update spec or tests.

EVID-3.statement: The design-proof MCP defines an append-only element grammar of EVIDENCE, RULE, PERMISSION, NECESSARY_CONDITION, RISK with grounding links and supersede semantics — usable as the decision-record vocabulary.

EVID-4.statement: execute-write tracks BASE_SHA and HEAD_SHA per task in session state but does not persist the task-to-commit mapping to any artifact. The raw material for traceability exists; the persisted artifact does not.

EVID-5.statement: plan-build invokes plan-attack (mandatory) and plan-smell (conditional) as the hardening gate. These are the closest existing analog in the field to spec-quality pressure-testing.

EVID-6.statement: Chester's plugin hook surface is SessionStart only. No PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PreCommit, or per-task hooks. Recently-reverted compaction hooks showed ambient hook infrastructure in Chester depends on platform surfaces that can shift.

EVID-7.statement: dbreunig's SDD Triangle (March 2026) identifies the structural diagnosis: SDD is a feedback loop, not a one-directional pipeline. Spec, tests, and code are three nodes whose coherence is maintained by continuous reconciliation.

EVID-8.statement: gotalab cc-sdd establishes spec-as-contract: spec owns observable behavior at component boundaries; implementation detail within boundaries belongs to code. This is the discipline that separates spec-level from plan-level discovery.

EVID-9.statement: Decision-record trigger criterion: a record is created precisely when two or more valid implementations exist and the existing spec/plan/tests do not determine which to choose. Not per-commit, not per-task — only when ambiguity surfaces.

EVID-10.statement: Decision records are append-only and supersede-capable. When a later sprint revisits a behavior, a new record references and supersedes the earlier one; neither is deleted. The chain is the archaeological record of how behavior evolved.

EVID-11.statement: Validation asymmetry across the three triangle relationships: Code↔Test is deterministic (tests pass or fail). Spec↔Test is structural (traceability, code-parseable, not LLM). Spec↔Code is probabilistic (LLM pressure-test only; the deterministic gate is the decision log itself).

EVID-12.statement: Within a single sprint, spec and plan form a hierarchy: spec is above plan. Spec-level discoveries propagate down and force plan changes; plan-level discoveries stay local and do not touch spec.

EVID-13.statement: Four-way bidirectional traceability: from spec clause (what decisions contributed), from test (what decision this validates), from code (what decisions ground this function), from decision (what spec/tests/code this touches). This is the queryability discipline that makes decision records load-bearing rather than decorative.

EVID-14.statement: A pending feature-definition brief (solution-design-language-kb, 2026-04-23) proposes a persistent proof-grammar-structured knowledge layer. Currently at proposal / phase-0 research stage, not implemented.

EVID-15.statement: Spec Kit (/speckit.reconcile) and OpenSpec (/opsx:sync) shipped drift-reconciliation commands since the April 2026 survey. Retrospective reconciliation at commit time is the current shipped competitor shape.

EVID-16.statement: Design-rationale capture systems (IBIS / gIBIS / QOC) have a 35-year adoption-failure record rooted in asymmetric cost — capture is expensive at author-time, benefit is deferred. LLM-in-the-loop may change the calculus but unproven.

RULE-1.statement: The persistent memory artifact lives at /docs/chester/decision-record as a flat markdown file, similar in form to memory.md. Separate from per-sprint artifacts; sibling to docs/chester/plans/ and docs/chester/working/.

RULE-2.statement: Design must anchor on the established prior art (dbreunig's SDD Triangle, gotalab spec-as-contract, and Chester's own architecture table from the context-handover doc) rather than re-deriving from external design-rationale history.

RULE-3.statement: Problem statement: Chester runs pipeline-shaped but works loop-shaped — implementation-surfaced decisions never propagate back to spec, tests, plans, and implementation, within the current sprint or for future sprints. Four destinations, two altitudes. Implementation is a destination because spec-level decisions surfaced mid-sprint may force retroactive revision of code already committed in earlier tasks, not just forward propagation.

RULE-4.statement: Both altitudes in scope: within-sprint loop closure (decision record updates spec, tests, and remaining plan tasks before task completion) and cross-sprint propagation (decisions reach into future sprints' plan-building). Layered lifecycle phases of the same atom, not separate features.

NCON-1.statement: Decision records must be created prospectively at the moment ambiguity surfaces during write-code — not retrospectively at sprint end. Prospective timing is what enables propagation to spec, tests, plans, and affected code before task completion.
NCON-1.collapse_test: Remove prospective timing: records become post-hoc reconstructions with no update path; the system collapses back to today's retrospective-only state and drift returns unaddressed.
NCON-1.reasoning_chain: IF records are captured only retrospectively (finish-write-records pattern reading the JSONL transcript), THEN spec and tests cannot be updated before the task commit lands and stay stale for the remainder of the sprint and every subsequent sprint. IF records are captured prospectively at ambiguity-surfacing during write-code, THEN spec, tests, plans, and affected code can be updated before task completion and the feedback loop closes within-sprint.
rejected_alternatives: Per-commit capture regardless of whether ambiguity surfaced

NCON-2.statement: Spec-level and plan-level discoveries must be discriminated mechanically (for example by test-skeleton pass/fail or contract-boundary check), not by LLM judgment alone.
NCON-2.collapse_test: Remove mechanical discriminator: the system either over-fires (every commit spawns a record, ceremony explodes) or under-fires (spec-level discoveries get silently resolved as plan-level).
NCON-2.reasoning_chain: IF every discovery is treated as spec-level, THEN ceremony fires on every implementation detail and the system dies the IBIS/gIBIS/QOC death of adoption-failure. IF every discovery is treated as plan-level, THEN spec-level discoveries never escalate and drift returns. IF the discrimination relies on LLM judgment alone, THEN the probabilistic gate violates the validation-asymmetry discipline and becomes advisory. IF a mechanical discriminator exists (test skeleton as arbiter, contract boundary check), THEN only boundary-questioning discoveries trigger propagation and ceremony cost scales with actual ambiguity.

NCON-3.statement: Every decision record must carry the full field set as defined in the record-structure rule — header (ID, Sprint, Task, Status, Tags) and body (Trigger, Context, Options Considered, Chosen, Rationale, Spec Update, Test, Code, Supersedes, Superseded By). Status carries one of Active, Superseded, or Abandoned.
NCON-3.collapse_test: Remove the field-set mandate: records become ad-hoc; some carry only outcome without rationale; four-way traceability breaks; six-month 'why does X behave this way?' has no answer — the failure mode the design exists to prevent.
NCON-3.reasoning_chain: IF a record omits any of the mandatory fields, THEN the record degrades toward a changelog entry (Chosen without Rationale, or Code without Spec Update); queries from any node in the four-way traceability graph fail to reach the full context; the record stops being load-bearing. IF the full field set is mandatory and authority-backed, THEN every record sustains bidirectional traceability and the rationale loss pattern that kills ADRs cannot fire.

NCON-4.statement: Propagation from a decision record to spec, tests, plans, and affected code must be a blocking gate on task completion — advisory propagation is insufficient.
NCON-4.collapse_test: Remove blocking gate: propagation becomes advisory; within weeks, records exist with stale spec and tests alongside them; drift is functionally identical to today.
NCON-4.reasoning_chain: IF propagation is advisory ('agent is encouraged to update spec'), THEN under time pressure, inattention, or model drift the propagation step gets skipped and the record-exists-but-artifacts-stale failure mode emerges, returning drift. IF propagation is a hard gate (task does not mark complete until spec, tests, and affected code align with the record), THEN propagation happens or the sprint stalls — drift is structurally impossible within-sprint.

NCON-5.statement: Propagation must reach backward into code already committed in earlier tasks of the current sprint — the 'implementation' destination named in the problem statement — not forward propagation only.
NCON-5.collapse_test: Remove retroactive reach: sprint ends with Task 1 code violating updated spec 2.3; the living-plan property fails within the sprint itself.
NCON-5.reasoning_chain: IF propagation is forward-only (remaining plan tasks and future sprints), THEN when Task 3 surfaces a spec-level decision that revises a clause Task 1 already implemented, Task 1's code stays unrevised and the sprint ends internally incoherent. IF propagation includes retroactive re-check of already-committed code against the updated spec, THEN within-sprint coherence holds throughout execution and finish-plan's coherence audit finds nothing to fix.

NCON-6.statement: The persistent decision store must live at /docs/chester/decision-record as a cross-sprint artifact, separate from per-sprint directories under plans/ or working/.
NCON-6.collapse_test: Remove central store: cross-sprint propagation dies; future sprints re-derive what prior sprints decided; outer loop stays broken.
NCON-6.reasoning_chain: IF decision records live only inside per-sprint subdirectories (plans/{sprint}/), THEN cross-sprint consultation requires sweeping the full archive every plan-build invocation — fragile, slow, and decisions effectively die with their origin sprint. IF records live in a central cross-sprint store at a known path, THEN future sprints consult them as a first-class named input to plan-build and outer-loop propagation is structurally enabled.
rejected_alternatives: Records duplicated in both per-sprint and central locations
Records embedded in the spec itself

NCON-7.statement: Supersede across sprints must be append-only with explicit bidirectional back-links — the superseded record is never rewritten or deleted.
NCON-7.collapse_test: Remove append-only discipline: the chain breaks on first supersede; 'why did this change?' becomes unanswerable; the supersede mechanism becomes a destructive update masquerading as versioning.
NCON-7.reasoning_chain: IF superseding a prior-sprint decision rewrites or deletes the original, THEN the archaeological chain breaks, rationale for the original choice is lost, and readers cannot reconstruct why the behavior evolved. IF supersedes are append-only with both the new record naming its predecessor and the old record carrying a forward link to its successor, THEN the chain preserves the full evolution and queries from any node reach the complete history.
rejected_alternatives: Implicit supersede detection (pattern matching) instead of explicit back-links

NCON-8.statement: plan-build must consult the persistent decision store when building each new sprint's plan, surfacing relevant prior decisions as named inputs that inform spec, plan, and implementation choices of the new sprint.
NCON-8.collapse_test: Remove plan-build consumption: records accumulate but never inform anything; outer-loop half of the problem statement goes unaddressed; cross-sprint drift is architecturally unsolved.
NCON-8.reasoning_chain: IF plan-build does not read the decision store, THEN records exist as write-only archive — the ADRs-nobody-reads failure mode. Cross-sprint learning does not compound; each sprint starts ignorant of what prior sprints learned. IF plan-build reads the store and surfaces relevant prior decisions (filtered by relevance, topic, or trace-query) as named inputs, THEN the outer loop closes and accumulated design truth becomes a first-class input to new work.

RISK-1.statement: Flat-file scale collapse at /docs/chester/decision-record: the persistent store grows unboundedly as records accumulate across sprints; past some threshold (hundreds of entries) the file becomes unreadable or token-prohibitive for plan-build consumption. Designer accepts this risk for now and will revisit when it grows.

RULE-5.statement: Decision record identifiers follow YYYYMMDD-XXXXX format where XXXXX is a zero-padded per-day sequence. The identifier doubles as the creation date — no separate Date field. Format is consistent with Chester's sprint-naming convention (YYYYMMDD-##-verb-noun-noun).

RULE-6.statement: Every decision record carries the following field set; no field is omitted (though some may carry 'none' with justification where appropriate). Header: ID (YYYYMMDD-XXXXX), Sprint, Task, Status (Active, Superseded, or Abandoned), Tags. Body: Trigger, Context, Options Considered, Chosen, Rationale, Spec Update (or 'none' with reason), Test (name + commit SHA), Code (file:line + commit SHA), Supersedes, Superseded By. Status becomes 'Superseded' when a later decision record explicitly supersedes this one; 'Abandoned' when the sprint that produced the record is abandoned without merge; otherwise 'Active'.

RULE-7.statement: Decision records are captured by the implementing agent autonomously when the trigger criterion fires (two or more valid implementations exist and existing artifacts don't determine). No human-approval gate intercepts record creation; the designer reviews records during standard review flow. Agent-autonomous capture preserves the non-interrupt discipline of the structured-decision approach.

NCON-9.statement: The mechanical discriminator between spec-level and plan-level discoveries is test-skeleton pass/fail. build-spec auto-scaffolds a test skeleton for each acceptance criterion at spec time; during write-code, a discovery is spec-level if implementation produces observable behavior that no existing skeleton covers, plan-level if implementation fails an existing skeleton (bug, not a decision) or produces behavior already covered (no decision to record).
NCON-9.collapse_test: Remove the test-skeleton mechanism without naming an alternative: discrimination falls back to LLM judgment (which condition #2 rejects) or designer judgment (which reintroduces interrupt ceremony that the agent-autonomous authority rule forbids). No viable fallback exists.
NCON-9.reasoning_chain: IF discrimination is left to narrative or LLM judgment, THEN condition #2's mechanical-discriminator requirement is unfilled and the system falls back to the failure modes condition #2 names (ceremony explosion or collapse). IF test skeletons are auto-scaffolded at spec time and serve as the discriminator, THEN (a) spec-test structural traceability required by the validation asymmetry is built in by construction, (b) discrimination becomes a checkable mechanical gate (skeleton covers this observable behavior, yes or no), and (c) condition #2's promise is made concrete without requiring a new artifact form.
rejected_alternatives: Contract-boundary check via explicit contract definitions (Chester does not have them; would require a separate large build)
Spec-criterion-ID reference by the implementer (recapitulates the judgment problem at the implementer level)
Designer-judges-each-time manual classification (conflicts with agent-autonomous authority rule)

NCON-10.statement: Decision records are stored direct at /docs/chester/decision-record from the moment of capture during write-code; no sprint-close promotion, distillation, or curation step. plan-build applies a filter at read time (by sprint subject match, tag, supersede-active-only, status = Active, or recency) rather than reading the entire store.
NCON-10.collapse_test: Remove direct-store-with-filter: the system falls back to one of the failing positions — sprint-scoped-then-promoted (classic ADR failure), dual-write (sync cost, divergence risk), or curated distillation (ADR-grade curation ceremony). All three are worse than the current position.
NCON-10.reasoning_chain: IF records require a curation or promotion step at sprint close, THEN the classic ADR adoption-failure mode fires — curation decays under time pressure, records accumulate unpromoted or are promoted-but-distorted, and the persistent memory rots from the inside. IF records are written direct to the cross-sprint store at capture time AND plan-build applies a filter at read rather than reading the whole file, THEN curation ceremony is eliminated (zero decay risk) while flat-file scale is managed at consumption rather than storage. The filter naturally excludes Abandoned-status and Superseded records, keeping plan-build input focused on currently-load-bearing decisions.
rejected_alternatives: Sprint-scoped then promoted at sprint close (classic ADR failure mode)
Dual-write — records exist in both sprint-scoped and cross-sprint locations (sync cost, divergence risk)
Curated distillation — designer reviews at sprint close and selectively promotes (ADR-grade ceremony, decays under pressure)
Read-whole-file at plan-build time (token budget collapse as the store grows)

RISK-2.statement: Abandoned sprint records persist in the cross-sprint store: when a sprint is abandoned (branch discarded, worktree closed without merge), records it wrote to /docs/chester/decision-record remain. Mitigated by the Status = Abandoned value (per revised RULE-6) which plan-build's read-time filter excludes from consideration alongside Superseded records. Records are preserved as historical artifact (real decisions at real moments) but do not pollute plan-building input.
